name: OpenAI o3-pro
archDisplay: Dense transformer reasoning model, extended test-time compute (exact size undisclosed)
note: Higher-compute o3 variant for the most reliable answers; replaced o1-pro.

results.notes: AIME 2024; OpenAI has not published a full official benchmark table for o3-pro [relabeled self-reported: aggregator/mirror relay of a vendor figure, not an independent re-measurement]
GPQA Diamond [relabeled self-reported: aggregator/mirror relay of a vendor figure, not an independent re-measurement]
Elo rating [relabeled self-reported: aggregator/mirror relay of a vendor figure, not an independent re-measurement]
SWE-bench Verified, thinking level high; DataLearner coding leaderboard (2026-07); serenitiesai.com lists 70.0 (conflict); no official OpenAI benchmark table exists for o3-pro

Aider polyglot code-editing benchmark (225 exercises across 6 languages, diff-edit format), 'o3-pro (high)' config; ranks 3rd behind GPT-5 variants. Independently cross-corroborated with an identical 84.9 figure on DataLearner's agent leaderboard (datalearner.com/en/leaderboards/category/agent).

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real. TechCrunch (fetched) confirms June 10, 2025 launch, ChatGPT Pro/Team + API availability, and $20/$80 per-1M pricing verbatim. developers.openai.com/api/docs/models/o3-pro confirms 200K context / 100K max output. Fetched binaryverseai.com directly and confirmed all three claimed numbers appear verbatim on the page: AIME 2024 '93 percent,' GPQA Diamond '84 percent,' Codeforces '2,748 points.' No changes made.

2026-07-16: Researched the six missing core benchmarks (mmlu, hle, math, humaneval, livecodebench, mmmu) and found no citable o3-pro-specific scores for any of them. Checked (1) official OpenAI sources: developers.openai.com/api/docs/models/o3-pro (fetched directly — confirms no benchmark scores are published on the page at all, only pricing/context specs) and the openai.com/index/introducing-o3-pro/ launch post (per secondary coverage, contains only human-preference-evaluation charts vs o1-pro/o3, no academic benchmark table); this matches the pre-existing note in this file that "OpenAI has not published a full official benchmark table for o3-pro." (2) Independent leaderboards: Epoch AI (epoch.ai/models/o3-pro shows only an aggregate Epoch Capabilities Index, no per-benchmark breakdown surfaced), Artificial Analysis (artificialanalysis.ai/models/o3-pro — its 9-eval Intelligence Index for o3-pro yields only an aggregate score of 33 plus a "display only" AA-GPQA Diamond figure of 84.5%; MMLU/MATH/HumanEval/LiveCodeBench are not part of that index at all, and no separate HLE or MMMU number for o3-pro could be found despite HLE being one of the index's 9 sub-evals), and BenchLM.ai, whose aggregator page explicitly states "2 of 313 tracked benchmarks are published" for o3-pro (the AA Intelligence Index and AA-GPQA Diamond, both already reflected above) — strong corroboration the six-benchmark absence is real rather than a search gap. Also checked DataLearner's coding leaderboard (no o3-pro row for LiveCodeBench) and vals.ai (no o3-pro model page exists there). (3) Sibling-model cross-reference: o3 (same family, April 2025 launch) does have its own published hle=24.9 and mmmu=82.9 in this corpus, but those are o3's own launch-day figures from a different, lower-compute model — o3-pro is a distinct June-2025 higher-compute variant that OpenAI never gave its own full benchmark table, so those numbers cannot be attributed to o3-pro without guessing. Conclusion: all six (mmlu, hle, math, humaneval, livecodebench, mmmu) are omitted_no_evidence — a legitimate absence, not a research failure, given OpenAI's decision not to publish a standard benchmark table for this "higher-reliability" variant.
